Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a Python dictionary representing a DECLARE model based on a specific scenario a complex multi department product design and launch process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Format A Python dictionary Structure Keys Constraint Types existence absence exactly one init responded existence coexistence response precedence succession altresponse altprecedence altsuccession chainresponse chainprecedence chainsuccession noncoexistence nonsuccession nonchainsuccession

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt text is slightly ambiguous but looking at standard pm4py DECLARE format binary constraints use A B tuples as keys I will assume the standard pm4py tuple format for binary constraints because mapping just an activity name for a binary constraint makes no sense semantically

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Scenario Activities IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Logic Constraints The Story I need to infer logical flow and rules from the description of a product design process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Start Usually starts with Idea Generation IG So init should probably be IG

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Existence All these activities likely happen at least once in a successful process instance So existence for all of them Or maybe some are optional Lets assume they are part of the main happy path or necessary branches

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Sequence Flow IG to DD DD to TFC and CE LT requires PC UT requires PC FL requires LT and UT and MP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Specific Constraints to Model Init IG Response If DD then eventually PC If PC then eventually FL If AG then FL Precedence DD requires IG TFC requires DD CE requires DD PC requires TFC and CE and AG LT requires PC UT requires PC MP requires AG FL requires LT and UT and MP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Coexistence TFC and CE often go together LT and UT often go together

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets select a robust subset of constraints to demonstrate the dictionary structure effectively

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets say AG is the gateway to Prototyping After PC we test LT UT Before FL we need MP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Formatting the Output Must be valid Python syntax dictionary Keys must match exactly the list provided in the prompt Values must be dictionaries Binary keys map A B to 1 0 1 0

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Key Format In pm4py specifically for existence etc the key is usually the activity name string For relations it is a tuple A B I will follow this convention

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Checking Constraint Logic against Scenario IG to DD makes sense DD to TFC CE parallel checks TFC CE to AG approval gate AG to PC prototype PC to LT UT testing AG to MP and MP to FL works well
